messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: @trisweb to answer to your question of paths in the other forum. You refer to a method to set up a pull-BOT. Also it looks like if the coding is mostly AI-generated which make it difficult to read for me. I am also not sure if the path reply is always correct (belonging to the ping message) because it is missing an essential condition to limit and what happen when lot of msg are received . I also would recommend in this situation (pull-BOT's) to use a sepearate #-channel like #test. But please correct me if I am wrong. I am still on a learning curve. If you solely want to know incoming RX-paths the following automation is sufficient: alias: Meshcore - Hops & Paths description: Create incoming paths triggers: - trigger: event event_type: meshcore_raw_event event_data: event_type: EventType.RX_LOG_DATA conditions: - condition: template value_template: "{{ trigger.event.data.payload.payload[0:2] | int(0) == 15 }}" actions: - variables: short: "{{ trigger.event.data.payload.payload }}" origin: "{{ short[0:2] | string }}" hops: "{{ short[2:4] | int(0) }}" start: "{{ (hops * 2 + 4 | int) }}" path: |- {% if hops > 0 %} {% set ns = namespace(s='') %} {% for i in range(hops) %} {% set b = short[4 + i*2 : 4 + i*2 + 2] %} {% set ns.s = ns.s + (('->' if ns.s else '') + b) %} {% endfor %} {{ ns.s }} {% else %} Direct {% endif %} mode: queued max: 10 You miss the step (action) what to do with the results; choose what ever you like. At the moment I cannot assign this path to a message, would love to, but this requires decode of the message (anyone?). And what about creating push-BOT's; Please move back to Meshtastic. (edited)

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: MC-HA dashboard for contact / discovery - display & management. Updated https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1435748802098237521/1455960944248356981 One of the reasons for this extra board was to not fill the main one with things that should not be there, like example dashboards full of yaml and images. So for anyone who may find it useful here are mine for for contact / discovery - display & management. This is fairly basic but very useful and not everyone knows how to do this kind of thing. Create a new view on a dashboard, click the edit pen, click the three dots in view configuration and edit in yaml. After sensibly examining it to make sure you can trust me - Drop the contents of one file and save Auto-Entities is nonstandard and needed https://github.com/thomasloven/hass-config/wiki/Lovelace-Plugins this is best installed with HACS https://hacs.xyz/ (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: The full path is always a good idea, special when your server has multiple USB-devices attached, on reboot they sometimes swap. The command to reveal this is sudo ls -la /dev/serial/by-id (but that part you already know). In docker (if using) you can rename it: devices: - /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Silicon_Labs_CP2102_USB_to_UART_Bridge_Controller_0001-if00-port0:/dev/ttyUSB_HELTECV2 The documentation of the integration itself is excellent, you might want to install my dashboard but please RTFM before you use the Manage Contacts (BULK) section..

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: @mrlm So I am happy that contacts with an default empty location (zero,zero) are not showing on the map. But you could always add them as a count. In above example I used badges for that. No rocket science but for laziness a badge example: type: custom:mushroom-template-badge content: >- {{ states.binary_sensor | selectattr('attributes.lastmod', 'defined') | selectattr('attributes.adv_lat', 'eq', 0 ) | selectattr('attributes.adv_lon', 'eq', 0 ) | list | count }} icon: mdi:globe-model color: red However because one person did a half fill (s)he placed his contact on the equator line (likely an error) but in the UK contacts can located themself on the Greenwich line (which is likely correct). Both types of locations, were either lat or lon is zero, should be on the map but are not. Was this your remark was about or did you also miss zero, zero?

messages.text: In order to set up the nodes just create an auto entities section. This could later be automated further to do this for you for each monitored node. type: grid cards: - type: heading heading: LS27-RPT MRLM - type: custom:auto-entities card: type: entities filter: include: - options: {} entity_id: sensor.meshcore_66aa4b2c7f_* exclude: [] grid_options: columns: 15 rows: auto column_span: 2 How I was doing it before was stupid, it's just so rare it changes it never annoyed me enough to redo it with auto-entities till now. (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: @👽 wazmac you can work with the visibility or exclude options of a card. for example below a visibility option were I only want to see the message channels of my focus. This is helpful to send a message in the intended channel. It works together with the original Mescore Messaging card. In the example below the result is focusing on some TEST channels; unfortunate people still think it is like MT and test a lot to see if it works, other people supports this with replying BOTS. Still awaiting a ping-pong battle between these BOTS. But here is the example: type: custom:auto-entities card: type: logbook title: Focus on TEST Channels filter: include: - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_ch_5_messages - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_ch_6_messages - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_ch_7_messages grid_options: columns: 18 rows: auto visibility: - condition: and conditions: - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_recipient_type state: Channel - condition: or conditions: - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_channel state: "#test (5)" - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_channel state: "#gyverbot (6)" - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_channel state: "#bot (7)" To make this card work in your dashboard you need to change/refer to the exact name/number of your channel(s). i.e. for Public you need only 1 condition with state Public (0) (edited)

In the example below the result is focusing on some TEST channels; unfortunate people still think it is like MT and test a lot to see if it works, other people supports this with replying BOTS. Still awaiting a ping-pong battle between these BOTS. But here is the example: type: custom:auto-entities card: type: logbook title: Focus on TEST Channels filter: include: - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_ch_5_messages - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_ch_6_messages - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_ch_7_messages grid_options: columns: 18 rows: auto visibility: - condition: and conditions: - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_recipient_type state: Channel - condition: or conditions: - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_channel state: "#test (5)" - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_channel state: "#gyverbot (6)" - condition: state entity: select.meshcore_channel state: "#bot (7)" To make this card work in your dashboard you need to change/refer to the exact name/number of your channel(s). i.e. for Public you need only 1 condition with state Public (0) (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: It is useless is this context; but if you extract the last hop from the path you get your companion's direct neighbors. Saving this neighbor ID (last 2 positions of path) as an entity like meshcore_snr_ID you can create the following graph. In this case reading all RX_LOG_DATA (not only adverts). This is helpful how well connected you are in the Mesh and perhaps changing the default setting of int.thresh (please correct me if I am wrong). Yes, your SNR values will be lower than your repeater ones (depending on both locations) but it is not creating unnecessary extra air-time, what you will do with any interval of telemetry_enabled on your repeater.

messages.author: megadimich
messages.text: How can you see in the HA integration that a message has been delivered? This applies to both private messages and channel messages.
messages.content_html: How can you see in the HA integration that a message has been delivered? This applies to both private messages and channel messages.

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: For private messages you use a combination of EventType.MSG_SENT and event.ACK , for channel messages you might check it with : trigger: event event_type: meshcore_raw_event event_data: event_type: EventType.RX_LOG_DATA payload: parsed: header: '15' decrypted: decrypted: true Not the nicest solution but you can check if your recent message bounces back from a repeater. But this reads ALL your incoming channels for ALL channel messages. You can not limit upfront on sender because you only have available a text field; a combi of <sender>: <message> but it is possible to limit on specific channel_idx: 0 . With a condition you need to limit on text or sender To extract sender this might be helpful: text[:text.find(":") . If you only need an occasional check work with: https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/channels?region=country

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: For example i can send in Developers Tool - Actions a Command provided by the MeshCore Python library and the result will be returned as an event (which is picked up by an automation): trigger: id: get_stats_radio idx: '3' alias: null platform: event event: event_type: meshcore_raw_event data: event_type: EventType.STATS_RADIO payload: noise_floor: -101 last_rssi: -66 last_snr: 12.5 tx_air_secs: 25 rx_air_secs: 167080 timestamp: 1769770260.1371098 This is not a good example, because the complete payload is already available as entities. but the event output is pretty short. In another event the output come automatic from the Meshcore Integration and includes path and SNR. This path exist out of repeaters (2 hex pos each) and I am interested in the last repeater because the SNR belongs to this one. From this repeater an entity needs to be created first, if not exist, before I can store the incoming SNR data. I got this working but my NOOB workaround is using MQTT -> Discover and this might be not the most efficient way to do it. I shortened the output of the event: platform: event event: event_type: meshcore_raw_event data: event_type: EventType.RX_LOG_DATA payload: snr: 12 rssi: -69 payload: >- payload_length: 86 parsed: header: '09' path_len: 32 path: 148878279b191868cdf4a82f77f5e934850a9deb2f89ce27491114171876f9cb So in this case the data is repeater=cb and SNR=12 and if entity does not exist I want to create a new entity meshcore_snr_cb and save the SNR. (edited)

It looks like the equivalent of an email alert that Exchange goes down, when you really want to send it out of band (by an alternative method to the system which has failed). Adding a push notification to a device you have installed the HA app on costs nothing, then you can also do this. alias: MeshCore - Direct message push notification description: Forwards Direct MeshCore messages to a push notification triggers: - trigger: event event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: direct conditions: [] actions: - action: notify.mobile_app_pixel_9_pro metadata: {} data: message: >- {% if trigger.event.data.channel is defined %} Channel {{ trigger.event.data.channel }}: {{ trigger.event.data.sender_name }}: {{ trigger.event.data.message }} {% else %} {{ trigger.event.data.sender_name }}: {{ trigger.event.data.message }} {% endif %} title: Meshcore Direct message via HA mode: single (edited)

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: Heltec v3 are cheap and reliable, Heltec v4 has a new version coming out v4.3 so I would order one from Aliexpress asking for this version. Inside the house ESP32 boards are best as the have WiFi but use more power. Heltec v4 has a poweramp to take it from 150mw to over 500mw, this is useful if you hace filters long cables or roof tiles that will weaken the signal. Outside the house nRF based boards use less power so fine for a solar repeater but but lack features storage / processing power of ESP32. (edited)

messages.text: Most of the time Home Assistant runs on one core of the CPU and needs less than 2GB of RAM. I only noticed this because I was suspicious of seeing 25% CPU usage. (edited)

messages.author: glowland ❧ NZ6K
messages.text: I ran into this too — the first two are free, then it won't make contacts out of additional repeaters. You have to move each one from the internal "discovered" list to the "contacts" list first. I made an 'entities' card thus: type: entities title: Manage Contacts entities: - entity: select.meshcore_discovered_contact name: Discovered secondary_info: last-changed - type: button name: ➕ Add Contact action_name: Add tap_action: action: call-service service: meshcore.add_selected_contact - type: button name: 🗑️ Remove Discovered action_name: Remove tap_action: action: call-service service: meshcore.remove_discovered_contact - entity: select.meshcore_added_contact name: Added secondary_info: last-changed - type: button name: ➖ Remove Contact action_name: Remove tap_action: action: call-service service: meshcore.remove_selected_contact and then on that card you use the "add contact" feature. then back in integration the repeaters should add ok. reference: https://meshcore-dev.github.io/meshcore-ha/docs/ha/contacts (edited)

awol
if it has a flaky route, yes. There is some tuning that can be done, I would say in general you are making a lot of requests and are probably saturating your request limit when connections are flakey with retries. I would suggest turning down the refresh rate and it will likely make things more stable
